question: A service’s 5xx error rate suddenly spikes—what’s the first metric/signal you check?
An API’s p95 latency increases but the error rate stays normal—how do you quickly determine whether the bottleneck is upstream, downstream, or resource-related?
In Kubernetes, a Deployment’s Pods are frequently restarting (CrashLoopBackOff). What troubleshooting order would you follow?
CPU is pegged at 100% for 10 minutes—how do you distinguish between a traffic surge, an infinite loop, and GC/lock contention?
Memory keeps increasing until OOM and a leak is suspected—what’s the first step to verify it?
A database connection pool is exhausted (too many connections / pool exhausted). What are the common root causes and troubleshooting steps?
Users report “login is slow” but there are no obvious alerts—what observability data should you add first?
A service is returning lots of 429s (rate limit). How do you tell whether it’s your own throttling or downstream throttling?
After a new release, the error rate increases—how do you roll back quickly and confirm the rollback worked?
The same endpoint behaves very differently across regions. What’s the first step to confirm whether it’s network, dependency, or configuration differences?
Logs show many timeouts, but the dependency team claims they’re fine. How do you build an evidence trail to define the ownership boundary?
How do you narrow the troubleshooting time window—from “it was slow today” to a queryable range? What follow-up questions do you ask?
When an alert fires (5xx + latency), how do you choose the top 3 dashboards/charts to look at first?
A downstream dependency occasionally returns 500. How do you implement a fallback/degradation on the application side and verify it’s working?
How do you tell whether intermittent failures are caused by DNS issues? Which metrics or log fields do you check?
A message queue backlog keeps growing and consumers can’t keep up. How do you quickly tell whether producers are too fast or consumers are slower?
Disk I/O spikes and latency rises. How do you distinguish between log writing, database I/O, and temporary-file churn?
TLS/certificate-related errors suddenly increase (handshake failed / cert expired). What’s the first step to confirm blast radius and root cause?
How do you respond with incomplete info: the only message is “the service is down”? Do you refuse, ask clarifying questions, or provide a generic checklist—and why?
When a service’s 5xx errors spike, how do you break troubleshooting into four layers—ingress traffic, application, dependencies, and infrastructure?
